zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Etton於1290年出生在英國越可爾吉爾林 易亞斯特時，她的父親Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill48歲，母親Mrs. Joan De Etton23歲。她於1319年與Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1380年在英國越可爾沃爾頓享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Etton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Elton (Berkshire, Cheshire, Derbyshire, Durham, Herefordshire, Huntingdonshire, Lancashire, and Nottinghamshire). The Eltons in Chesire, Derbyshire, and Durham are from Old English ǣl ‘eel’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. Elton in Berkshire is from the Old English female personal name Æthelflǣd (composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ + flǣd ‘beauty’). Both Elton in Herefordshire and in Nottinghamshire are from the Old English personal name Ella + tūn. The Berkshire origin for the surname is especially well represented.
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in eastern Norway, in Old Norse Elgjartún ‘enclosed sacred place’ (or fenced in for elg ‘moose’?).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Etton was born in 1290, in Gilling East, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill, was 48 and her mother, Mrs. Joan De Etton, was 23. She married Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton in 1319. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1380, in Walton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth d’Etton est née en 1290, à Gilling East, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Ivo d’Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Joan De Etton, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton en 1319. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1380, à Walton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Etton 1290 in Gilling East, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill 48 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Joan De Etton 23. Sie hat 1319 Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1380 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Walton, Yorkshire, England.

Lei sposato Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton nel 1319. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1380, nel Walton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 90 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Ettonが1290年にイギリスヨークシュアイアギリング エアストで生まれた時，父Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhillは48歳，母Mrs. Joan De Ettonは23歳であった。1319年にSir Thomas Fairfax II of Waltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1380年にイギリスヨークシュアイアワルトンで90歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Etton(이)가 1290년 영국 요르크실르 길링 이스트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Joan De Etton(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1319년 Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1380년 영국 요르크실르 월턴에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Etton родилась в 1290 г. в местности джиллинг эаст, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Joan De Etton, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Fairfax II of Walton в 1319 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1380 г. в местности валтон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет.
